danger_signals.visual_form: HUD display in first-person cockpit view showing hostile contacts
Unique enemy entity appearing in opening plot sequence
Some factions attack on sight; visual differentiation of hostile vs. neutral ships
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat danger based on faction allegiance

danger_signals.visual_form: Designated enemy ships with homicidal behavioral patterns

safety_signals.visual_form: Terran Confederation and merchant faction ships
Transition from cockpit view to static overview/first-person room views
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; combat-free environment for trading and interaction

safety_signals.visual_form: Menu interface on planets and bases
safety_signals.meaning: Legitimate business opportunity, structured game progression

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Attack behavior; player can choose to become pirate

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Trade stations, commodity prices displayed on planets/bases
Sector defense operations, law enforcement missions

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Homicidal behavior pattern, distinctive enemy ships
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hidden headquarters; associated with Governor Menesch weapons trafficking

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mysterious drone appearing in opening systems; plot-critical unique enemy entity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Drone destroys ships indiscriminately; escalating danger level signals plot progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ancient ship with powerful weapon visible in exploration; artifact as inventory item
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Steltek scout pursues player; drone follows after weapon acquisition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Terran Confederation requests player assistance; ambush coordination missions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Confederation offers military contracts; world state shifts to crisis response

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Righteous Fire expansion - weapon theft state
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stolen Steltek weapon triggers plot escalation; Retro faction activity increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Governor Menesch revealed as antagonist; Retro weapon copies indicate distributed threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Faction-specific reactions vary; mission availability tied to faction standing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Some factions attack on sight if standing is poor; others grant access to fixers and missions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Commodity prices displayed on planet/station menus; price differentials visible in interface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Profit margins drive player movement between systems; trading creates economic geography

environmental_storytelling.example: Man who hires player on New Detroit is found dead; reveals larger conspiracy without exposition
Mysterious artifact functions as visual/inventory object that drives entire plot; connects to Steltek civilization
Exploration reveals Steltek technology location; visual evidence of advanced dead civilization
Retro headquarters location revealed through informant; physical location embodies faction identity
Governor Menesch selling weapons to pirates and extremists; environmental evidence of systemic instability
Righteous Fire adds new threat (Retros) and escalates stakes; world state visibly becomes more dangerous

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Destroys everything encountered; indiscriminate aggression; follows player after weapon acquisition
Pursues player for weapon information; energizes ancient weapon in exchange for location data
Attack on sight based on faction affiliation and player reputation; combat simulation follows Wing Commander conventions
Provide missions, trade, or ignore player depending on standing; non-hostile unless provoked
Represent specific factions; assign faction-aligned missions in exchange for money/information; appear on planets/bases
Orchestrates weapon theft; supplies extremists and pirates; functions as conspiracy figurehead

notes: Privateer communicates world-state primarily through faction interaction patterns and visual HUD displays in cockpit view. The game uses sandbox mechanics where player reputation dynamically shapes how factions manifest hostile or cooperative behavior. Danger signals are minimal in visual design (pseudo-3D raster at 320×200) but communicate through entity behavior and HUD data. The mysterious drone and Steltek artifacts serve as visual MacGuffins that drive narrative world-state progression. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on NPC dialogue and mission context rather than visual design, making faction identity dependent on gameplay interaction rather than distinctive visual markers. The Righteous Fire expansion escalates visual threat signals through increased hostile faction presence and weapon acquisition stakes.
